findings.description: find_user_by_email runs SELECT * FROM users, materializes every row into User objects, then linear-scans in Python to find one email — an O(n) full-table load where an indexed WHERE email = ? is O(1).

Eagerly building the full all_users list before scanning allocates one dataclass per row in the table; even with the bad query you could short-circuit in the cursor loop instead of materializing everything first.

The 100k-iteration PBKDF2 (correct security cost) is computed while the DB connection opened on line 55 stays open, needlessly extending connection hold time and serializing logins under the connect-per-call model — do NOT lower the iteration count.
_connect opens a fresh sqlite3 connection per call with no pooling/reuse; cheap for SQLite but pure per-request setup overhead and wasteful if ever pointed at a networked DB.
Out of my lane (defer to critic-security): email is f-string-interpolated into the SQL, a clear injection hole; any perf fix here must be parameterized regardless.
Out of my lane (defer to critic-logic): the expiry comparison is inverted — it deletes/expires live sessions and returns already-expired ones as valid.
